pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 Requirements: System Components (Modules) - Confluence; CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Form Designer Introduction. Official documentation lists 'Form Designer' not 'MakeView'.
CDC Epi Info 7 Requirements: System Components (Modules) - Confluence
CDC Epi Info 7 Requirements: System Components (Modules) - Confluence; CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Chapter 9 - Classic Analysis

pi_items.verified_value: HBsAg (Hepatitis B Surface Antigen) is a core serological marker used in HBV surveillance and is part of the standard triple panel for HBV testing
pi_items.source: CDC Hepatitis B Surveillance Guidance; CDC Clinical Testing and Diagnosis for Hepatitis B; Hepatitis B Foundation - Understanding Your Test Results. HBsAg detection in two clinical specimens >6 months apart is part of the case definition for chronic hepatitis B.

pi_items.verified_value: Anti-HBc (Antibody to Hepatitis B Core Antigen) is a standard component of HBV serological testing. Total anti-HBc indicates previous or ongoing HBV infection and persists for life.
pi_items.source: CDC Hepatitis B Surveillance Guidance; CDC Clinical Testing and Diagnosis for Hepatitis B. Anti-HBc appears at the onset of symptoms in acute hepatitis B and is part of the standard HBV testing panel.

pi_items.verified_value: HBeAg (Hepatitis B e-Antigen) is a marker for viral replication and infectivity used in HBV surveillance. Detection of HBeAg in two clinical specimens >6 months apart is part of surveillance case definition.
pi_items.source: CDC Hepatitis B Surveillance Guidance; Mayo Clinic Laboratories - HBeAg Overview; Hepatitis B Foundation - Understanding Your Test Results

pi_items.metadata_value: SourceOfInfection (Text, 50 chars) - includes injection drug use, sexual contact, unknown, perinatal, healthcare setting
pi_items.verified_value: Hepatitis B transmission occurs through three main modes: parenteral/percutaneous (including injection drug use and healthcare-related), sexual contact, and perinatal transmission. In the US, injection drug use and multiple sexual partners are the most commonly reported risk exposures.
pi_items.source: CDC Hepatitis B Surveillance Guidance; CDC 2023 Hepatitis Surveillance Report; WHO Hepatitis B Fact Sheet; NIH Epidemiology and Prevention of Hepatitis B Virus Infection

pi_items.verified_value: Vaccination status is relevant to HBV surveillance. CDC recommends all infants receive hepatitis B vaccine at birth, with full series recommended. Categories of Unvaccinated, Partial, and Complete represent realistic vaccination statuses.
pi_items.source: CDC Hepatitis B Vaccine Administration; CDC Hepatitis B Perinatal Vaccine Information; Johns Hopkins - Why Hepatitis B Vaccination Begins at Birth

pi_items.verified_value: These are valid case classifications for Hepatitis B surveillance. CDC surveillance systems track acute HBV infection, chronic HBV infection, and perinatal HBV infection as distinct reportable conditions.
pi_items.source: CDC 2023 Hepatitis Surveillance Report; CDC Hepatitis B Surveillance Guidance; CDC Viral Hepatitis Surveillance And Case Management

pi_items.key: required_fields - other (CaseID, ReportDate, County, Sex, AgeAtDiagnosis)
pi_items.metadata_value: Standard epidemiological data: case ID, report date, geographic location, demographics, age
pi_items.verified_value: These are standard core data elements for disease surveillance per CDC NNDSS guidelines: age, race/ethnicity, sex, date of onset, date of report, and county of residence are core reportable data.
pi_items.source: CDC 2023 Hepatitis B Surveillance Report; CDC NNDSS - National Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System
Task specification - no external verification possible

pi_items.metadata_value: ROUTEOUT command used with REPLACE option and file path
pi_items.verified_value: ROUTEOUT is a real Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis command that directs output to a named file. REPLACE keyword deletes existing file before writing.
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Command Reference: ROUTEOUT

pi_items.verified_value: FREQ is a real Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis command for frequency tables. Syntax: FREQ [<variable(s)>]
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Command Reference: FREQ; Display Statistics

pi_items.verified_value: MEANS is a real Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis command that computes descriptive statistics for continuous numeric variables. Syntax: MEANS <variable>
pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Command Reference: MEANS

privileged_info_summary: Hepatitis B is a real notifiable disease in the United States with CDC-mandated surveillance involving acute, chronic, and perinatal cases. The required form fields (HBsAg, Anti-HBc, HBeAg, source of infection, vaccination status, clinical status) are authentic epidemiological markers used in real HBV surveillance systems. The transmission sources (injection drug use, sexual contact, perinatal transmission) represent documented real-world HBV transmission routes. Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis commands (ROUTEOUT, FREQ, MEANS) are verified real commands. However, the module name 'MakeView' is incorrect—the actual Epi Info 7 form creation module is named 'Form Designer' per official CDC documentation. The Enter module and Classic Analysis module names are correct. The task uses synthetic data (agent must create 8 case records, not pull from real datasets).
